task_completion.visual_evidence: The eQUEST application is open with '4StoreyBuilding' visible in the title bar, project tree, or recent files list.

task_completion.description: Locate and select windows on East and West facing exterior walls
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent navigates the Building Shell component tree or spreadsheet view and selects WINDOW objects within zones containing 'E' or 'W' in their names (e.g., G.E12, M.W24).

task_completion.description: Set the left vertical shading fin depth to 2.5 feet for target windows
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the window properties dialog (Shading tab) or spreadsheet view, the 'Left Fin' (or LEFT-FIN-H) dimension is set to 2.5 for the selected East/West windows.

task_completion.description: Set the right vertical shading fin depth to 2.5 feet for target windows
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the window properties dialog (Shading tab) or spreadsheet view, the 'Right Fin' (or RIGHT-FIN-H) dimension is set to 2.5 for the selected East/West windows.
The eQUEST simulation calculation dialog (DOE-2 execution) is visible, or a 'Simulation Complete' / results viewing prompt appears on screen.
The agent clicks the Save icon (floppy disk), uses File -> Save, or a save confirmation dialog is visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the eQUEST graphical user interface to make changes rather than directly editing the .INP text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with eQUEST dialog boxes, component trees, and spreadsheets, rather than a text editor (like Notepad) modifying the 4StoreyBuilding.inp file.

integrity.description: The agent did not add fins to North or South facing windows.
integrity.visual_evidence: If North or South windows (zones with 'N' or 'S') are visible in the properties or spreadsheet views, their Left/Right Fin dimensions remain empty or 0.

integrity.description: The simulation was actually executed within the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard eQUEST/DOE-2 simulation progress window with calculation steps is shown, rather than a mocked terminal command or bypassed execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target fin depth is exactly 2.5 feet. Target orientations are strictly East and West (zones containing 'E' or 'W' such as G.E12, M.W24). North and South windows must NOT have fins added. The software used is eQUEST 3.65. Window fin properties are modified in the Building Shell module and are typically labeled as 'Left Fin' and 'Right Fin' in the GUI (corresponding to LEFT-FIN-H and RIGHT-FIN-H in the INP file). The project file is located at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\eQUEST 3-65 Projects\4StoreyBuilding\4StoreyBuilding.inp.
